Title: A synthetic switch based on orange carotenoid protein to control blue–green light responses in chloroplasts

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Orange carotenoid protein family, OCP2; N-terminal domain (NTD) and C-terminal domain (CTD)
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast stroma when fused to plastid localization sequence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synthetic transgenes expressed in mesophyll protoplasts and stable transgenic seedlings/leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Split NanoLuc complementation assay in protoplasts and stable plants; light wavelength/intensity/time-course assays; plastid versus cytosolic localization comparison
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables a keto-carotenoid-dependent, reversible blue–green light photoswitch in chloroplasts; dark promotes module association and luminescence, light promotes dissociation and loss of signal
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required prosthetic pigments for OCP2 module assembly and photoconversion
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; green light; white light; red light; darkness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fremyella thermalis OCP2 domains were split and used as a synthetic chloroplast photoswitch. 2. In Arabidopsis, switch function required plastid localization and keto-carotenoid production. 3. Blue/green light dissociated the modules, while darkness rapidly restored activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary study focus is synthetic optogenetic control rather than endogenous nutrient metabolism, but OCP2 function depends causally on keto-carotenoid availability.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual background interaction in wild type and incomplete light suppression; activation under normal white/blue photoperiods may limit tight control.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can serve as a plastid optogenetic module or keto-carotenoid-responsive biosensor in metabolically engineered chloroplasts.

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/chloroplast via RBCS-3 plastid localization sequence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression in Arabidopsis lines under 35S promoter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic overexpression; RT-qPCR of transgene; HPLC carotenoid profiling; thylakoid pigment quantification; phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces keto-carotenoid biosynthesis in Arabidopsis, producing canthaxanthin and predominantly astaxanthin, thereby enabling OCP2-based photoswitch function
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Serve as prosthetic pigments for OCP2 assembly/photoconversion; keto-carotenoids also have photoprotective/antioxidant relevance
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth light regime; light-dependent photoswitch assays in crtW background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Plastid-targeted crtW expression generated keto-carotenoid-producing Arabidopsis plants. 2. Astaxanthin accumulated more than canthaxanthin, and production reduced neoxanthin/violaxanthin pools. 3. crtW expression was essential to confer functionality to the OCP2 synthetic switch and OCP2-based transcription control.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although an enzyme, in this paper crtW serves mainly as an enabling metabolic engineering component for the synthetic light switch rather than as the central mechanistic subject of pathway dissection.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered carotenoid composition and slightly browner pigmentation, though no significant plant size penalty was reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to engineer keto-carotenoid accumulation in plastids and to support carotenoid-dependent optogenetic systems or biosensors.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed fusion protein in agroinfiltrated leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient agroinfiltration with Sig2-CTD and NTD-AsiA fusions; RT-qPCR of target gene Atcg00150 under dark versus green light; comparison in crtW versus wild type
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: When fused into the OCP2 switch, Sig2-mediated chloroplast transcription is repressed in darkness and restored by green light in keto-carotenoid-producing plants
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Light-dependent reversible inhibition/release of sigma factor activity via induced protein association/dissociation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes Atcg00150 expression when released from AsiA under green light; repressed in dark when associated with AsiA through OCP2 modules
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with plastid-encoded RNA polymerase (PEP); engineered association with AsiA via OCP2 modules
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of plastid transcription in the synthetic circuit
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sig2 was fused to OCP2 CTD to build a chloroplast light-controlled transcription module. 2. Coexpression with NTD-AsiA repressed the Sig2 target Atcg00150 in darkness. 3. Green light restored target expression specifically in crtW plants, demonstrating keto-carotenoid-dependent switch control.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sig2 is not studied here as a native regulator of nutrient metabolism but as a synthetic control element downstream of the OCP2 switch.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential limitation to plastids capable of supporting the engineered keto-carotenoid-dependent interaction; broader plastid transcription side effects not deeply assessed.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be adapted to light-gated chloroplast transgene or plastid gene expression systems.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Green light 350 µmol m^-2 s^-1 for 4 h; darkness
